sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content: Surgery plays a limited role in MM management, primarily for diagnosis, stabilizing fractures, or relieving spinal cord compression. It is not a primary treatment for MM but may be part of multimodality approach for solitary plasmacytomas or complications.

cards.name: Orthopedic fixation
cards.detail:
Approach: For impending or actual long-bone fractures or vertebral compression fractures
Indications: Structural instability, fracture risk, or spinal cord compression [MYEL-I].
Key Complications: Infection, hardware failure, delay in systemic therapy [MYEL-D].
Operative Mortality: Low in elective procedures; higher in emergency settings. Not specified in document.

cards.name: Vertebroplasty or kyphoplasty
cards.detail:
Approach: Minimally invasive procedure for vertebral compression fractures
Indications: Symptomatic vertebral compression fractures [MYEL-I].
Key Complications: Cement leakage, infection, new fracture [MYEL-I].
Operative Mortality: Low; not specified in document.

cards.name: Debulking surgery
cards.detail:
Approach: For solitary plasmacytomas causing neurologic compromise or mass effect
Indications: Solitary plasmacytoma with spinal cord compression or neurological compromise [MYEL-2].
Key Complications: Bleeding, infection, neurological deficit [MYEL-2].
Operative Mortality: Variable based on site and patient status; not specified in document.
